evals.prompt:
Create a BCOS newsletter from this event data. Here's the pipeline:

Event | Date & Time | Type | PIC | Status
---
Dear HSBA Members, Join us for Hong Kong Trivia Night! Time: 7:45pm April 21, 2026. Venue: Slippery Slope @ 31A Keong Saik Rd (2nd floor). Cost: SG$40 nett (incl. 1 drink). Register by April 17. Registration: https://alt.jotfor.ms/260841695038463 | April 21, 2026 7:45pm | Collab | Chris | Marketing
Rewind: A 90s Social Afternoon. UCL Culture Club curated 90s-themed social for alumni across schools including UCL, Yale, INSEAD, NYU, LBS, Penn/Wharton, Columbia, Oxford, Cambridge, Cornell, Berkeley. Date: Saturday 2 May 2026, 11am-3pm. Venue: Havelock. Ticket: $68 incl lunch. Registration: https://luma.com/1qsytns3 | May 2, 2026 11am-3pm | Collab | Erica | Marketing
Dean Jennifer Chatman (BA 81, PhD 88) discusses the human edge of innovation at Haas. Hosted by Ong Tze Boon at BeyondX. Friday 8th May, 18:00-20:00. Beyond X, 21 Keppel Rd. RSVP: https://web.cvent.com/event/4eeccb1d-2802-4997-a5a6-745f4f2d8c89/register | May 8, 2026 6-8pm | Signature | Chris | Marketing
Pickleball Night | Early May | Signature | Erica | Planning
Wellness Talk with Columbia University | Mid May | Signature | Erica | Planning
Board Member Prep for BCOS Members | May/June | Signature | Chris | Planning
Super AI | May/June | Signature | Erica | Planning
Berkeley AGM + Annual Gala | 14th Nov | Signature | Erica & Chris | Planning
evals.expected_output: Two files: (1) HTML email newsletter with Dean Chatman as featured event, HSBA Trivia + Rewind as event cards, 4 teaser events in On the Horizon, and Gala as Save the Date. (2) WhatsApp text summary with same content structure, emojis, raw links, under 1500 chars.
assertions: HTML file is saved to /mnt/user-data/outputs/bcos-newsletter.html
WhatsApp file is saved to /mnt/user-data/outputs/bcos-whatsapp.txt
HTML uses Berkeley Blue #003262 and Gold #FDB515
HTML has inline styles only (no <style> blocks)
HTML contains all 3 RSVP/registration links
HTML contains Instagram, LinkedIn, Website links in footer
HTML contains Chris Fong and Erica Ding LinkedIn links
HTML contains membership sign-up link
HTML has Featured Event section with Dean Chatman
HTML has event cards for HSBA Trivia and Rewind
HTML has On the Horizon section with Pickleball, Wellness, Super AI, Board Prep
HTML has Save the Date for AGM + Gala Nov 14
WhatsApp uses *bold* formatting (not Markdown **bold**)
WhatsApp contains raw URLs (not hyperlinked text)
WhatsApp includes membership link
WhatsApp is under 2000 characters

evals.prompt:
Hey, we have a new round of events for BCOS. Can you make the newsletter and WhatsApp blast?

Event | Date | Type | PIC | Status
---
Networking Dinner at Burnt Ends. 20 seats only, $150pp. June 12 7pm. RSVP: https://forms.gle/abc123 | June 12, 2026 7pm | Signature | Chris | Marketing
Pickleball Social with Stanford Club. June 20, 4-7pm. East Coast Park. $25. Register: https://luma.com/pickle123 | June 20 4-7pm | Collab | Erica | Marketing
Berkeley AGM + Annual Gala | 14th Nov | Signature | Erica & Chris | Planning
evals.expected_output: Two files: (1) HTML with Networking Dinner as featured (Signature + exclusive), Pickleball as event card, Gala as Save the Date. (2) WhatsApp summary, short and punchy.
assertions: HTML file is created and presented
WhatsApp file is created and presented
HTML Featured Event is the Networking Dinner (Signature type, exclusive/intimate)
HTML has event card for Pickleball Social
HTML has Save the Date for AGM + Gala
No 'On the Horizon' teasers section since only Gala is in Planning and it's a Save the Date
WhatsApp includes both event details with raw links
Both files use correct BCOS branding and fixed links

evals.prompt: Create the BCOS newsletter from this: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xOAJ7F949EGqE7_kaLQNC29q46rapQYvsSBWa9Y161A/edit?gid=0#gid=0
evals.expected_output: Claude fetches the Google Sheet, parses events, and generates both HTML newsletter and WhatsApp summary. If the sheet doesn't load properly, Claude asks the user to paste or upload the data instead of failing silently.
assertions: Claude attempts to fetch the Google Sheets URL
If fetch succeeds: Claude produces both HTML and WhatsApp outputs
If fetch fails or data is unreadable: Claude asks user to paste data or upload CSV
Claude does not hallucinate event details not present in the data
HTML follows the BCOS template structure
WhatsApp follows the BCOS WhatsApp format
